missing.find_3_equivalent_pairs_on_the_fractio: Find 3 equivalent pairs on the fraction wall
missing.find_an_equivalent_set_with_the_common: Find an equivalent set with the common-denominator grid
missing.serve_25_equal_plates_in_plate_rush: Serve 25 equal plates in Plate Rush
missing.position_on_a_number_line_great_for_ma: Position on a number line (great for magnitude)
missing.finding_the_common_denominator_step_by: 🔍 Finding the common denominator step by step
missing.take_the_highest_power_of_each_prime_t: Take the highest power of each prime that appears:
missing.divide_top_and_bottom_by_the_gcd: Divide top and bottom by the GCD:
missing.terminating_decimal_denominator_only_h: ✓ Terminating decimal (denominator only has prime factors 2 and 5)
missing.repeating_decimal_parentheses_mark_the: ↻ Repeating decimal (parentheses mark the repeating block)
missing.look_at_the_shaded_pieces_how_many_pie: Look at the shaded pieces. How many pieces are filled?
missing.click_any_piece_to_highlight_equivalen: 🧱 Click any piece to highlight equivalent fractions. Click two pieces to check if they are equivalent!
missing.different_visual_models_highlight_diff: Different visual models highlight different aspects of fractions. Pie shows part-of-whole, number line shows magnitude, area shows multiplication, set shows discrete groups.
missing.ask_the_student_how_many_apples_in_all: Ask the student: "How many apples in all?" →
missing.move_from_physical_objects_to_a_drawn_: Move from physical objects to a drawn diagram. The same fraction, but now as a visual model.
missing.the_pie_and_bar_both_show: The pie and bar both show
missing.shaded_the_whole_is_split_into_equal_p: shaded. The whole is split into equal parts. The fraction names how many of those parts.
missing.now_use_only_the_written_symbol_the_st: Now use only the written symbol. The student sees the connection between the symbol and the concrete/representational forms.
missing.captures_the_same_idea_as_the_apples_a: captures the same idea as the apples and as the pie.
missing.the_numerator_is_how_many_parts_we_hav: The numerator is how many parts we have; the denominator is how many parts make a whole.
missing.bruner_s_cra_framework_is_the_textbook: Bruner's CRA framework is the textbook approach for math instruction in special education.
missing.move_through_three_stages_with_the_sam: Move through three stages with the same fraction to build conceptual understanding before procedural fluency.
missing.fractions_make_sense_when_they_connect: Fractions make sense when they connect to the world. Pick a context and a grade level to find problems that match your interests.
missing.common_core_standards_k_8_fraction_tra: 📋 Common Core Standards — K-8 fraction trajectory
missing.each_tool_in_fraction_lab_is_mapped_to: Each tool in Fraction Lab is mapped to one or more CCSS standards. Use this view to plan instruction, find tools for a specific standard, or build evidence for IEP goal alignment.
missing.a_reference_library_of_the_12_most_doc: A reference library of the 12 most-documented fraction misconceptions, each with research-grounded remediation strategies.
missing.click_any_misconception_to_expand_its_: Click any misconception to expand its full description, why it happens, and what to do about it.
missing.customers_will_order_fractional_pizzas: Customers will order fractional pizzas. Slice the pizza into the right number of pieces and serve the right amount.
missing.earn_tips_serve_10_customers_to_win: Earn tips. Serve 10 customers to win!
missing.see_a_visual_fraction_type_it_as_fast_: See a visual fraction. Type it as fast as you can. Score as many as possible in 30 seconds!
missing.find_pairs_of_equivalent_fractions_6_p: Find pairs of equivalent fractions. 6 pairs total. Try to do it in as few moves as possible.
missing.each_round_shows_a_target_fraction_and: Each round shows a target fraction and 4 fish. Catch the one that matches.
missing.you_have_3_lives_wrong_catches_lose_a_: You have 3 lives. Wrong catches lose a life.
missing.combine_fraction_strips_to_make_exactl: Combine fraction strips to make exactly 1. Choose pieces that add to the whole.
missing.no_pieces_yet_add_from_below: No pieces yet. Add from below.
missing.see_a_sequence_of_fractions_what_comes: See a sequence of fractions. What comes next?
missing.5_5_bingo_card_with_fractions_call_a_f: 5×5 bingo card with fractions. Call a fraction, then mark any cell with an equivalent fraction. Get 5 in a row to win!
missing.two_fractions_face_off_pick_which_is_l: Two fractions face off. Pick which is larger to pull the rope. 5 correct pulls = win the tug!
missing.tap_the_bigger_fraction_to_pull: Tap the bigger fraction to pull!
missing.a_fraction_is_hiding_use_the_clues_to_: A fraction is hiding. Use the clues to guess what it is. Fewer hints = more points.
missing.house_rule_every_guest_at_a_table_gets: House rule: every guest at a table gets an EXACTLY equal plate. Divide each dish into the table's number of equal shares, then read off the fraction each guest gets.
missing.show_read_it_pick_choose_the_fraction_: Show = read it; Pick = choose the fraction; Type = type it (hardest). Auto-set by level; change anytime.
missing.zen_mode_has_no_timer_and_no_game_over: Zen mode has no timer and no game-over. A missed plate in Timed mode just loops back around.
missing.no_stumbles_every_serve_was_fair: No stumbles - every serve was fair.
missing.a_record_of_what_was_practiced_not_a_m: A record of what was practiced, not a measure of ability.
missing.those_groups_are_not_equal_every_guest: Those groups are not equal - every guest must get the same.
missing.how_much_does_each_guest_get: How much does each guest get?
missing.type_how_much_each_guest_gets: Type how much each guest gets:
missing.any_equal_form_counts_2_8_is_the_same_: Any equal form counts - 2/8 is the same as 1/4.
missing.share_dishes_into_equal_plates_for_tab: Share dishes into equal plates for tables of guests. Zen (no-timer) default.
missing.serve_customers_fractional_pizzas_earn: Serve customers fractional pizzas. Earn tips. 10 customers to win.
missing.type_the_fraction_you_see_beat_the_30_: Type the fraction you see. Beat the 30-second clock.
missing.memory_pairing_find_6_pairs_of_equival: Memory pairing — find 6 pairs of equivalent fractions.
missing.catch_the_fish_whose_fraction_matches_: Catch the fish whose fraction matches the target. 3 lives.
missing.5_5_card_mark_equivalent_fractions_to_: 5×5 card. Mark equivalent fractions to get 5-in-a-row.
missing.pick_the_larger_fraction_to_pull_the_r: Pick the larger fraction to pull the rope. First to 5 wins.
missing.guess_the_hidden_fraction_using_progre: Guess the hidden fraction using progressive clues.
missing.combine_fraction_strips_to_make_exactl_2: Combine fraction strips to make exactly 1.
missing.identify_the_next_fraction_in_a_sequen: Identify the next fraction in a sequence.
missing.place_fraction_discs_in_a_7_6_grid_two: Place fraction discs in a 7×6 grid. Two players.
missing.four_games_practice_different_fraction: Four games practice different fraction skills. Pizza Shop drills part-of-whole intuition. Race drills speed. Match drills equivalence. Fish drills magnitude comparison.
missing.generate_printable_practice_worksheets: Generate printable practice worksheets with answer keys. Pick a topic, number of problems, and print.
missing.include_answer_key_on_a_separate_page: Include answer key on a separate page
missing.snapshot_of_student_practice_in_this_s: Snapshot of student practice in this session. Useful as supplementary evidence for IEP/RTI documentation.
missing.definitions_of_every_fraction_term_use: Definitions of every fraction term used in K-8 math instruction, with examples. Searchable.
missing.pre_formatted_smart_goals_for_iep_team: Pre-formatted SMART goals for IEP teams. Each goal links to a CCSS standard, suggests progress-monitoring data sources within AlloFlow, and lists common accommodations. Copy and adapt for your student.
missing.ready_to_use_micro_units_3_5_days_each: Ready-to-use micro-units (3-5 days each) for common fraction topics. Each plan ties activities to specific Fraction Lab tabs so you can demo or assign directly.
missing.the_earliest_fraction_notation_dates_t: The earliest fraction notation dates to ancient Egypt around 1800 BCE. The Egyptians used "unit fractions" — only fractions with numerator 1 (like 1/2, 1/3, 1/4). They wrote any other fraction as a sum of unit fractions: 2/5 = 1/3 + 1/15. The modern bar notation (numerator over denominator) comes from medieval Arabic mathematicians around the 1200s CE.
missing.where_do_the_fraction_words_come_from: Where do the fraction words come from?
missing.numerator_comes_from_the_latin_numerar: "Numerator" comes from the Latin "numerare" (to count) — it counts how many parts. "Denominator" comes from "denominare" (to name) — it names the size of the parts. Together: the denominator names what kind of pieces; the numerator counts how many of those pieces you have.
missing.decimals_as_we_know_them_were_populari: Decimals as we know them were popularized by Simon Stevin in 1585 in his book "De Thiende" (The Tenth). Before that, fractions were the only way to express non-whole numbers. The decimal point became universally adopted only in the 1700s.
missing.percent_comes_from_the_latin_per_centu: "Percent" comes from the Latin "per centum" — "per hundred." So 25% literally means 25 per 100, or 25/100. The % symbol evolved from the abbreviation "per c." in 15th-century Italian commercial writing.
missing.the_reciprocal_of_a_b_is_b_a_flip_it_u: The reciprocal of a/b is b/a — flip it upside down. Every nonzero fraction has a reciprocal. The product of a fraction and its reciprocal is always 1: (3/4) × (4/3) = 12/12 = 1. This is why dividing by a fraction is the same as multiplying by its reciprocal.
missing.1_2_is_the_most_common_fraction_in_eve: 1/2 is the most common fraction in everyday speech because it sits exactly in the middle of 0 and 1. It is also the only fraction we routinely express in words: "half" instead of "one over two." Other languages have unique words for 1/3 and 1/4 too (third, quarter).
missing.a_pizza_is_a_perfect_fraction_model_be: A pizza is a perfect fraction model because it is a circle (so all slices radiate from the center), the slices are easy to count, and the model maps onto the formal mathematical definition: a fraction n/d is n equal parts out of d total equal parts.
missing.some_numbers_like_and_2_cannot_be_writ: Some numbers, like π and √2, cannot be written as a fraction of two whole numbers. These are called irrational numbers. The Pythagoreans (around 500 BCE) reportedly killed the mathematician Hippasus when he proved that √2 could not be written as a fraction.
missing.any_real_number_can_be_written_as_a_co: Any real number can be written as a "continued fraction" — a nested fraction like 1 + 1/(2 + 1/(2 + 1/(2 + ...))). This gives the best fraction approximations. π ≈ 22/7 from the first few terms; even better is 355/113.
missing.musical_pitch_ratios_are_fractions_the: Musical pitch ratios are fractions. The octave is a 2:1 ratio (frequency doubles). The perfect fifth is 3:2. The major third is 5:4. Pythagoras discovered that simple integer ratios produce harmonious sounds.
missing.two_pizzas_can_only_be_combined_if_the: Two pizzas can only be combined if they are cut into the same-size slices. Different denominators = different-size slices = can't directly combine. The common denominator literally means "same-size pieces."
missing.a_batting_average_like_333_means_1_3_o: A batting average like .333 means 1/3 of at-bats result in a hit. A pitcher's ERA, on-base percentage, slugging percentage — all of them are fractions. Baseball was the first sport to popularize this kind of statistical thinking, starting in the 1850s.
missing.georges_cuisenaire_was_a_belgian_prima: Georges Cuisenaire was a Belgian primary teacher in the 1950s who invented colored rods of length 1 to 10 to help children grasp number relationships including fractions. The "fraction wall" you see in many classrooms is the digital descendant of his physical rods.
missing.the_chinese_mathematical_text_nine_cha: The Chinese mathematical text "Nine Chapters on the Mathematical Art" (around 100 BCE) included fraction arithmetic, including a procedure for finding common denominators that is mathematically equivalent to what we teach today.
missing.computers_store_decimal_fractions_in_b: Computers store decimal fractions in binary. The decimal 0.1 cannot be represented exactly in binary — it becomes a repeating fraction in base 2. This is why 0.1 + 0.2 sometimes gives 0.30000000000000004 in programming languages.
missing.dogs_spend_about_1_2_their_lives_sleep: Dogs spend about 1/2 their lives sleeping. A 12-year-old dog has slept about 6 years.
missing.cats_sleep_about_2_3_of_every_day_that: Cats sleep about 2/3 of every day. That's 16 hours daily.
missing.elephants_drink_about_1_8_of_their_bod: Elephants drink about 1/8 of their body weight in water per day. A 12,000-pound elephant drinks 1,500 lbs of water!
missing.a_blue_whale_s_tongue_weighs_about_1_2: A blue whale's tongue weighs about 1/2 of a small car (~6,000 lbs).
missing.a_bee_visits_about_1_500_flowers_to_ma: A bee visits about 1,500 flowers to make 1/12 teaspoon of honey.
missing.a_giraffe_s_neck_is_about_1_3_of_its_t: A giraffe's neck is about 1/3 of its total height.
missing.a_snail_moves_at_about_1_40_of_a_mile_: A snail moves at about 1/40 of a mile per hour. It would take 40 hours to crawl 1 mile.
missing.a_kangaroo_can_leap_a_distance_about_3: A kangaroo can leap a distance about 3/2 of its body length in one hop.
missing.a_dolphin_sleeps_with_half_its_brain_a: A dolphin sleeps with half its brain at a time — only 1/2 of the brain rests while the other half stays alert.
missing.a_giant_tortoise_can_live_to_150_years: A giant tortoise can live to 150+ years. A 100-year-old tortoise has lived 2/3 of its potential lifespan.
missing.real_world_fraction_facts_from_the_ani: Real-world fraction facts from the animal kingdom. Use as journal prompts, attention-grabbers, or cross-curricular tie-ins to science.
missing.alloflow_fraction_lab_stands_on_the_sh: AlloFlow Fraction Lab stands on the shoulders of decades of mathematics education research and practice.
missing.robert_siegler_and_colleagues_for_the_: Robert Siegler and colleagues for the IES Practice Guide that shaped this tool's emphasis on magnitude reasoning and the number-line model.
missing.jerome_bruner_1966_for_the_original_en: Jerome Bruner (1966) for the original enactive-iconic-symbolic progression. Sealander et al. for evidence in special education contexts.
missing.georges_cuisenaire_1952_for_the_rod_sy: Georges Cuisenaire (1952) for the rod system that became the fraction wall and fraction strip models.
missing.vamvakoussi_vosniadou_stafylidou_lorti: Vamvakoussi, Vosniadou, Stafylidou, Lortie-Forgues, and many others whose careful documentation of student thinking made the Misconceptions tab possible.
missing.sherry_parrish_for_the_routine_that_in: Sherry Parrish for the routine that informed the Math Talks tab.
missing.cast_for_the_udl_guidelines_that_shape: CAST for the UDL guidelines that shape every feature decision.
missing.mathematics_is_the_music_of_reason_jam: "Mathematics is the music of reason." — James Joseph Sylvester
missing.version_history_of_alloflow_fraction_l: Version history of AlloFlow Fraction Lab. The v3.0 expansion brought it to its current size.
missing.2_player_game_place_discs_that_show_ra: 2-player game. Place discs that show random fractions in the 7×6 grid. Have fun naming the fractions.
missing.pick_one_number_talk_string_students_e: Pick one Number Talk string. Students explain reasoning out loud.
missing.show_3_4_fractions_in_the_pie_model_st: Show 3-4 fractions in the pie model. Students identify or describe.
missing.walk_through_concrete_representational: Walk through Concrete → Representational → Abstract for current topic.
missing.curated_practice_items_at_student_s_le: Curated practice items at student's level.
missing.pick_a_hard_puzzle_discuss_strategies: Pick a hard puzzle. Discuss strategies.
missing.read_a_real_world_tool_example_aloud_d: Read a real-world tool example aloud. Discuss.
missing.pre_built_sequences_of_activities_that: Pre-built sequences of activities that fit common time blocks (10 min, 30 min, 45 min). Pick one and follow it.
missing.generate_a_3_question_exit_ticket_on_t: Generate a 3-question exit ticket on the topic just taught. Print and hand out at the end of the lesson.
missing.per_grade_skills_checklists_use_during: Per-grade skills checklists. Use during one-on-one assessment or to plan instruction.
missing.very_common_fractions_break_the_rules_: Very common. Fractions break the rules students learn for whole numbers — bigger numbers don't always mean bigger values. The frustration is real and normal. Help by: (1) using visual models like pizzas or fraction strips, (2) connecting to real life (cooking, time, money), (3) practicing little and often (5 min a day) rather than long sessions, (4) celebrating effort, not just correct answers.
missing.my_child_doesn_t_understand_why_proced: My child doesn't understand WHY procedures work
missing.they_are_not_alone_most_students_learn: They are not alone — most students learn the procedure first and the why later. Help by always showing what the procedure looks like with a picture. "Multiply 3/4 by 1/2" can be drawn as a rectangle. The Op Proofs tab does this for you. Ask "why?" gently and frequently.
missing.how_can_i_help_with_homework_without_d: How can I help with homework without doing it for them?
missing.use_the_worked_examples_tab_they_show_: Use the Worked Examples tab — they show step-by-step procedures with reasoning. Walk through similar problems together, not the exact same one. If they say "I don't know how to start," ask "What does the problem ask you to find? What do we already know?" The hint system in Word Problems gives layered hints — try the first one before the answer.
missing.ies_practice_guide_recommends_1_magnit: IES Practice Guide recommends: (1) magnitude reasoning (number line work), (2) connections between visual and symbolic, (3) word problems in context. AVOID: drill on procedures without understanding. Spend 5 minutes on Benchmark Trainer, 5 on Word Problems, and 5 on a game — that's a great 15-minute session.
missing.my_child_gets_the_procedure_right_but_: My child gets the procedure right but the answer wrong
missing.usually_means_a_small_arithmetic_error: Usually means a small arithmetic error (e.g., multiplied wrong) or didn't simplify. Show them the Cheat Sheet — they can check their work step by step. Encourage them to verify with a different method (estimate first, or use a different model).
missing.what_if_i_don_t_remember_fractions_wel: What if I don't remember fractions well myself?
missing.you_don_t_have_to_be_the_expert_use_th: You don't have to be the expert! Use the Tab Guide and Worked Examples — they explain procedures clearly. Better: model "let's figure this out together." You can learn alongside your child. The Vocabulary tab is helpful even for adults.
missing.how_do_i_know_if_my_child_is_on_track: How do I know if my child is on track?
missing.look_at_the_standards_tab_to_see_which: Look at the Standards tab to see which CCSS standards they should know for their grade. The Mastery tab shows progress. For a quick check: can they (a) draw a model for 3/4, (b) say a fraction equivalent to 1/2, (c) compare 2/3 and 3/4 and explain? Those three tests cover most K-5 fraction expectations.
missing.guidance_for_parents_helping_kids_with: Guidance for parents helping kids with fractions at home. Common challenges, practical tips, and what to ask.
missing.the_why_of_learning_recruiting_interes: The "WHY" of learning — recruiting interest, sustaining effort, self-regulation
missing.the_what_of_learning_perception_langua: The "WHAT" of learning — perception, language, comprehension
missing.the_how_of_learning_physical_action_ex: The "HOW" of learning — physical action, expression, executive function
missing.every_fraction_lab_feature_mapped_to_a: Every Fraction Lab feature mapped to a Universal Design for Learning (UDL) guideline from CAST.
missing.use_this_to_articulate_to_administrato: Use this to articulate to administrators or grant reviewers how AlloFlow operationalizes UDL.
missing.the_foundational_ies_practice_guide_5_: The foundational IES Practice Guide. 5 recommendations including the central use of the number-line model.
missing.origin_of_the_enactive_concrete_iconic: Origin of the enactive (concrete) → iconic (representational) → symbolic (abstract) progression that becomes CRA.
missing.cra_in_special_education_context_evide: CRA in special education context. Evidence for sequencing.
missing.origin_of_the_translation_model_moving: Origin of the translation model — moving between symbolic, visual, verbal, manipulatives, and real-world contexts.
missing.maps_how_students_construct_fraction_m: Maps how students construct fraction magnitude over time.
missing.synthesizes_the_cognitive_challenges_o: Synthesizes the cognitive challenges of fraction arithmetic.
missing.foundational_text_for_the_number_talk_: Foundational text for the Number Talk routine.
missing.growth_mindset_applied_to_mathematics_: Growth mindset applied to mathematics education.
missing.original_cuisenaire_rod_system_that_be: Original Cuisenaire rod system that became the prototype for fraction strips.
missing.meta_analysis_showing_positive_effects: Meta-analysis showing positive effects of manipulatives on math achievement.
missing.effects_of_ed_tech_integration_on_math: Effects of ed-tech integration on math learning.
missing.worked_example_effect_why_showing_solu: Worked-example effect — why showing solutions can be more effective than practice alone for novices.
missing.the_research_base_for_alloflow_fractio: The research base for AlloFlow Fraction Lab. Use these citations when justifying tool use to administrators, in IEP documentation, or in grant applications.
missing.use_the_slider_to_change_numerator_and: Use the slider to change numerator and denominator. The pie/bar updates live.
missing.toggle_between_pie_and_number_line_to_: Toggle between pie and number-line to build flexibility.
missing.plot_multiple_fractions_to_see_their_r: Plot multiple fractions to see their relative size.
missing.don_t_skip_stages_linger_on_concrete_u: Don't skip stages. Linger on Concrete until confident.
missing.click_two_rows_to_compare_their_height: Click two rows to compare their heights directly.
missing.use_the_search_to_find_a_specific_term: Use the search to find a specific term.
missing.don_t_skip_the_explanation_steps_read_: Don't skip the explanation steps — read each one.
missing.read_aloud_as_class_discussion_prompts: Read aloud as class discussion prompts.
missing.different_from_vocab_tab_more_terms_mo: Different from Vocab tab — more terms, more examples.
missing.pick_one_entry_per_week_as_a_journal_p: Pick one entry per week as a journal prompt.
missing.have_students_try_to_make_exactly_1: Have students try to make exactly 1.
missing.use_the_credits_as_evidence_base: Use the credits as evidence base.
missing.show_the_visual_model_alongside_the_sy: Show the visual model alongside the symbolic comparison.
missing.try_chains_of_length_20_to_drill_the_p: Try chains of length 20 to drill the pattern.
missing.try_the_same_value_in_all_representati: Try the same value in all representations.
missing.daily_warm_up_1_minute_of_benchmark_qu: Daily warm-up — 1 minute of benchmark questions.
missing.no_timer_browse_at_your_pace: No timer. Browse at your pace.
missing.don_t_let_students_use_without_first_a: Don't let students use without first attempting by hand.
missing.take_untimed_first_then_under_time_pre: Take untimed first, then under time pressure.
missing.set_a_low_daily_target_5_problems_for_: Set a low daily target (5 problems) for sustainability.
missing.hints_are_progressive_encourage_studen: Hints are progressive — encourage students to try first.
missing.rotate_games_don_t_over_rely_on_one: Rotate games — don't over-rely on one.
missing.pair_with_a_hands_on_activity_real_rec: Pair with a Hands-on Activity (real recipe).
missing.don_t_do_all_in_one_sitting: Don't do all in one sitting.
missing.present_as_a_trick_first_explain_after: Present as a "trick" first; explain after.
missing.bring_in_actual_rulers_and_measuring_c: Bring in actual rulers and measuring cups.
missing.download_as_txt_attach_to_docs: Download as txt, attach to docs.
missing.use_as_templates_for_your_own: Use as templates for your own.
missing.color_blind_palette_helps_many_student: Color-blind palette helps many students with visual processing.
missing.walk_through_one_strategy_per_session: Walk through one strategy per session.
missing.print_before_the_school_day_starts: Print before the school day starts.
missing.pick_the_closest_profile_not_a_perfect: Pick the closest profile, not a perfect match.
missing.use_to_give_students_feedback_on_reaso: Use to give students feedback on reasoning.
missing.cross_reference_with_your_district_pac: Cross-reference with your district pacing guide.
missing.each_tab_includes_its_purpose_when_to_: Each tab includes its purpose, when to use it, and a pro tip.
missing.useful_for_new_teachers_substitute_tea: Useful for new teachers, substitute teachers, or anyone evaluating the tool.
missing.egyptian_unit_fractions_1_n_and_specia: Egyptian unit fractions (1/n) and special notations for 2/3. They wrote any fraction as a sum of distinct unit fractions: 2/5 = 1/3 + 1/15.
missing.babylonians_used_base_60_for_fractions: Babylonians used base 60 for fractions. This is why we still have 60 minutes in an hour and 360 degrees in a circle.
missing.pythagoreans_believed_all_numbers_were: Pythagoreans believed all numbers were ratios of whole numbers. Hippasus reportedly proved √2 is irrational and was thrown overboard.
missing.indian_mathematicians_develop_place_va: Indian mathematicians develop place-value notation that will eventually enable modern decimal arithmetic.
missing.comprehensive_treatment_of_fractions_i: Comprehensive treatment of fractions including reduction to lowest terms.
missing.systematic_treatment_of_fractions_in_h: Systematic treatment of fractions in his Arithmetic — introduces algorithms for the four operations.
missing.fibonacci_introduces_hindu_arabic_nume: Fibonacci introduces Hindu-Arabic numerals and modern fraction notation (line between top and bottom) to Europe.
missing.simon_stevin_advocates_decimal_fractio: Simon Stevin advocates decimal fractions for everyday calculation. The decimal point as we know it follows.
missing.napier_s_logarithms_make_fraction_arit: Napier's logarithms make fraction arithmetic tractable for astronomy and navigation.
missing.the_decimal_point_becomes_universal_ac: The decimal point becomes universal across Western mathematics.
missing.modern_american_math_curriculum_solidi: Modern American math curriculum solidifies around the procedure-first model that the IES Practice Guide later critiques.
missing.georges_cuisenaire_invents_colored_fra: Georges Cuisenaire invents colored fraction rods. Becomes the prototype for fraction strips and the "fraction wall" model.
missing.siegler_et_al_publish_developing_effec: Siegler et al. publish "Developing Effective Fractions Instruction K-8." Recommends number-line model as central.
missing.ccss_math_k_8_fraction_trajectory_beco: CCSS-Math K-8 fraction trajectory becomes the de facto national framework, with explicit attention to magnitude reasoning.
missing.open_source_agpl_licensed_fraction_ins: Open-source, AGPL-licensed fraction-instruction tool combining all major pedagogical frameworks in one platform.
missing.4_000_years_of_fraction_history_from_e: 4,000 years of fraction history, from Egyptian unit fractions to modern computational tools.
missing.show_the_same_idea_visually_verbally_a: Show the SAME idea visually, verbally, and physically (dual coding) — this helps ALL students. It does NOT mean a student is a single "visual / auditory / kinesthetic" type; that meshing claim is unsupported (Pashler 2008).
missing.predictable_routines_limit_visual_clut: Predictable routines. Limit visual clutter. Allow extra processing time.
missing.short_bursts_movement_breaks_embed_nov: Short bursts. Movement breaks. Embed novelty.
missing.build_slowly_with_concrete_materials_u: Build slowly with concrete materials. Use multiple representations.
missing.show_fraction_vocabulary_in_home_langu: Show fraction vocabulary in home language alongside English. The math is universal.
missing.push_depth_density_infinity_continued_: Push depth: density, infinity, continued fractions. Avoid acceleration without depth.
missing.pick_a_learning_profile_to_see_which_f: Pick a learning profile to see which Fraction Lab tabs and accommodations are most helpful.
missing.these_are_suggestions_not_prescription: These are suggestions, not prescriptions — every student is unique.
missing.set_a_personal_daily_and_weekly_target: Set a personal daily and weekly target. Track your progress. Sustained practice beats intensity for retention.
missing.quotes_about_mathematics_learning_and_: Quotes about mathematics, learning, and the beauty of numbers. Use as classroom posters, journal prompts, or just for inspiration.
missing.favorite_sports_in_a_class_of_30: Favorite Sports in a Class of 30
missing.fractions_are_how_we_describe_parts_of: Fractions are how we describe parts of a data set. Each survey shows real-looking data and asks fraction questions about it.
missing.pick_a_misconception_walk_through_its_: Pick a misconception. Walk through its remediation strategies step by step. Use this when a student is consistently making a specific error.
missing.generate_a_customizable_worksheet_with: Generate a customizable worksheet with answer key.
missing.time_bounded_probe_for_rti_progress_mo: Time-bounded probe for RTI progress monitoring.
missing.all_fraction_operations_on_one_page: All fraction operations on one page.
missing.print_lab_everything_printable_in_one_: 🖨 Print lab — everything printable in one place
missing.quick_access_to_every_printable_artifa: Quick access to every printable artifact in Fraction Lab. Use for offline practice, classroom posters, or take-home materials.
missing.a_page_of_fraction_strips_from_1_whole: A page of fraction strips from 1 whole to 1/12. Cut out and use for any concrete-representational activity.
missing.developing_effective_fractions_instruc: "Developing Effective Fractions Instruction for Kindergarten Through 8th Grade." 5 evidence-based recommendations.
missing.vamvakoussi_vosniadou_stafylidou_lorti_2: Vamvakoussi & Vosniadou; Stafylidou; Lortie-Forgues et al.
missing.pie_bar_number_line_area_set_length_vo: Pie, bar, number line, area, set, length, volume models.
missing.visit_the_standards_tab_for_the_comple: Visit the Standards tab for the complete cross-reference.
missing.alloflow_is_open_source_agpl_v3_develo: AlloFlow is open-source (AGPL v3). Developed by Aaron Pomeranz, PsyD. Built with AI-assisted development.
missing.click_pieces_to_add_them_to_your_works: Click pieces to add them to your workspace. Try to make patterns, build wholes, or explore equivalence visually.
missing.no_pieces_yet_add_some_from_above: No pieces yet. Add some from above.
missing.track_your_progress_on_each_fraction_s: Track your progress on each fraction skill. Each skill has a target — keep practicing until you hit 100%.
missing.you_have_half_of_a_pizza_your_friend_h: You have half of a pizza. Your friend has 1/4 of a pizza. Together you have more than 1/2 of a whole pizza. Why?
missing.sarah_ate_1_3_of_a_pie_tom_ate_1_3_of_: Sarah ate 1/3 of a pie. Tom ate 1/3 of the same pie. How much of the pie is left?
missing.a_fraction_has_these_properties_its_nu: A fraction has these properties: its numerator is 1 less than its denominator, and it equals 0.8. What fraction is it?
missing.if_the_denominator_is_d_the_numerator_: If the denominator is d, the numerator is d - 1. So (d - 1) / d = 0.8.
missing.i_take_1_2_of_a_cake_then_i_take_1_2_o: I take 1/2 of a cake. Then I take 1/2 of what's left. Then 1/2 of what's STILL left. How much have I taken total?
missing.take_1_2_leaves_1_2_take_1_2_of_that_1: Take 1/2, leaves 1/2. Take 1/2 of that = 1/4. Then 1/2 of remaining 1/4 = 1/8.
missing.fill_in_this_3x3_magic_square_so_each_: Fill in this 3x3 magic square so each row, column, and diagonal sums to 3/2. One cell shows 1/2.
missing.in_a_magic_square_the_middle_cell_equa: In a magic square the middle cell equals the magic sum ÷ 3. Each row sums to 3/2.
missing.half_of_half_of_half_is_what_fraction: Half of half of half is what fraction?
missing.5_friends_share_3_ropes_equally_how_mu: 5 friends share 3 ropes equally. How much rope does each friend get?
missing.in_a_race_you_have_run_3_8_of_the_dist: In a race, you have run 3/8 of the distance. Your friend has run 1/3 less than you. How much has your friend run?
missing.your_friend_ran_3_8_1_3_of_the_distanc: Your friend ran 3/8 − 1/3 of the distance.
missing.a_pizza_is_cut_into_12_slices_1_4_of_t: A pizza is cut into 12 slices. 1/4 of the pizza has pepperoni. 1/3 has mushrooms. 1/6 has both. The rest has just cheese. What fraction has just cheese?
missing.inclusion_exclusion_pepperoni_mushroom: Inclusion-exclusion: pepperoni + mushrooms − both = total with toppings. The rest is cheese only.
missing.each_term_is_half_the_previous_this_is: Each term is half the previous. This is a geometric series.
missing.10_fraction_puzzles_ranging_from_easy_: 10 fraction puzzles ranging from easy to hard. Each has a hint and a worked solution.
missing.quick_guide_to_using_alloflow_fraction: Quick guide to using AlloFlow Fraction Lab.
missing.learn_practice_apply_teacher_group_rel: (Learn, Practice, Apply, Teacher) group related work.
missing.within_each_mode_lets_you_pick_specifi: within each mode lets you pick specific tools.
missing.shows_your_score_and_streak_across_all: shows your score and streak across all tabs.
missing.is_always_available_for_asking_questio: is always available for asking questions about what you're doing.
missing.visualization_heavy_build_intuition_wi: Visualization-heavy. Build intuition with pie, bar, number-line, area, set, length, and volume models. No quiz pressure.
missing.skill_focused_with_scoring_compare_add: Skill-focused with scoring. Compare, add/subtract, multiply/divide, equivalent fractions, simplify, convert, and timed quizzes.
missing.real_world_contexts_word_problems_reci: Real-world contexts. Word problems, recipe scaling, games (Pizza Shop, Race, Match, Fish, and more), story mode, brain teasers.
missing.instructor_tools_worksheet_generator_p: Instructor tools: worksheet generator, progress reports, IEP goal bank, lesson plans, CCSS standards alignment, misconceptions library, scope & sequence, hands-on activities, RTI probes.
missing.ask_the_ai_tutor_any_question_about_fr: Ask the AI tutor any question about fractions or about how to use a particular tab.
missing.the_vocabulary_and_glossary_tabs_cover: The Vocabulary and Glossary tabs cover every term in K-8 fraction instruction.
missing.for_teachers_the_misconceptions_and_fa: For teachers: the Misconceptions and FAQ tabs answer common student confusions.
missing.alloflow_fraction_lab_is_open_source_s: AlloFlow Fraction Lab is open-source software (AGPL v3) built by Aaron Pomeranz, PsyD.
missing.pedagogical_framework_drawn_from_the_i: Pedagogical framework drawn from the IES Practice Guide (Siegler et al., 2010), Bruner's CRA framework, and Cuisenaire's fraction strip model (1950s).
missing.ccss_standards_from_corestandards_org_: CCSS standards from corestandards.org. Misconception library compiled from research by Vamvakoussi & Vosniadou, Stafylidou, Lortie-Forgues et al., and others.
missing.reference_tables_showing_all_common_fr: Reference tables showing all common fractions with their decimal and percent equivalents. Use to look up exact values or to spot patterns.
missing.comprehensive_fraction_glossary_with_e: Comprehensive fraction glossary with examples. Covers vocabulary from K-8 fraction instruction.
missing.number_talk_strings_parrish_2010_numbe: Number-talk strings (Parrish, 2010 — "Number Talks") for fraction reasoning. 5 prompts in a sequence with a common pattern.
missing.15_minute_teacher_led_discussion_focus: 15-minute teacher-led discussion focused on mental math and student explanation.
missing.see_a_definition_pick_the_term_master_: See a definition; pick the term. Master fraction vocabulary.
missing.generate_a_printable_reference_card_wi: Generate a printable reference card with fractions, decimals, and percents. Tape it to a binder or desk for quick lookup.
missing.physical_off_screen_activities_to_teac: Physical, off-screen activities to teach fractions concretely. Print-friendly. Each activity has materials, steps, an extension, and CCSS alignment.
missing.suggested_month_by_month_pacing_for_fr: Suggested month-by-month pacing for fraction instruction at each grade level. Use this to map AlloFlow tools to your curriculum calendar.
missing.a_4_point_holistic_rubric_for_evaluati: A 4-point holistic rubric for evaluating student work on fraction problems. Each level has criteria and an example response. Useful for portfolio assessment and conferencing.
missing.how_much_flour_do_you_need_in_total: How much flour do you need in total?
missing.how_much_should_the_customer_pay: How much should the customer pay?
missing.how_much_of_a_cup_do_you_each_get: How much of a cup do you each get?
missing.how_many_sticks_of_butter_do_you_need: How many sticks of butter do you need?
missing.do_you_have_enough_sugar_for_tomorrow_: Do you have enough sugar for tomorrow? If so, how much will be left after baking?
missing.a_5_chapter_story_where_each_chapter_i: A 5-chapter story where each chapter is a fraction problem in context. Work through the bakery day to apply your skills.
missing.multiple_choice_quizzes_that_mirror_st: Multiple-choice quizzes that mirror standardized test format. Pick a grade level, take a 10-question test, see your score.
missing.a_short_daily_ritual_5_problems_per_da: A short daily ritual: 5 problems per day. Build a streak. Practice schedules beat practice intensity for retention.
missing.let_your_number_be_n_the_sequence_n_1_: Let your number be n. The sequence: (n+1) × 2 + 4 − 6 = 2n + 2 + 4 − 6 = 2n. Divide by 2 → n. Subtract original → 0. Wait — that gives 0, not 1. Try: n → (n+1)×2 = 2n+2 → +4 = 2n+6 → −6 = 2n → ÷2 = n → −original = 0. The "trick" relies on hidden constants in the sequence.
missing.each_term_is_half_the_previous_the_tot: Each term is half the previous. The total approaches 1 but never quite reaches it. This is an infinite geometric series with first term 1/2 and ratio 1/2: S = (1/2) / (1 − 1/2) = 1. This is why Zeno's paradox of motion is solvable mathematically.
missing.even_though_each_term_is_small_the_ser: Even though each term is small, the series diverges. To pass 100, you need about 1.5 × 10^43 terms. This was proven by Nicole Oresme in the 1300s using grouping: 1/3 + 1/4 > 1/4 + 1/4 = 1/2; 1/5+...+1/8 > 4 × 1/8 = 1/2; you can always find another 1/2 to add.
missing.check_1_4_and_1_3_4_1_1_3_1_check_2_5_: Check 1/4 and 1/3: (4)(1) − (1)(3) = 1. Check 2/5 and 1/2: (5)(1) − (2)(2) = 1. This property generates the Stern-Brocot tree of all rational numbers.
missing.rewrite_1_2_2_3_3_4_n_1_n_the_2_in_2_3: Rewrite: 1/2 × 2/3 × 3/4 × ... × (n−1)/n. The 2 in 2/3 cancels the 2 in 1/2's denominator. The 3 in 3/4 cancels the 3 in 2/3's denominator. Everything cancels except 1/n.
missing.mathematical_tricks_that_are_really_el: Mathematical "tricks" that are really elegant fraction identities. Pick one to see the setup, the reveal, and the mathematical explanation.
missing.a_standard_inch_ruler_is_divided_into_: A standard inch ruler is divided into 16ths. Major lines: inch. Half mark: 1/2 inch. Quarter mark: 1/4 inch. Each smallest segment: 1/16 inch.
missing.inch_rulers_use_binary_fractions_halve: Inch rulers use binary fractions: halves, quarters, eighths, sixteenths.
missing.a_12_foot_tape_measure_has_marks_every: A 12-foot tape measure has marks every 1/16 inch. Common usage: read to the nearest 1/8 or 1/16. The black diamonds every 19 3/16 inches mark engineered joist spacing.
missing.each_16_inches_has_a_black_diamond_for: Each 16 inches has a black diamond for typical stud spacing.
missing.a_standard_set_includes_1_cup_1_2_1_3_: A standard set includes 1 cup, 1/2, 1/3, 1/4. Some sets add 2/3 and 3/4. Glass measuring cups have markings for 1/4, 1/3, 1/2, 2/3, 3/4 cup and 1 cup.
missing.you_can_combine_smaller_measures_to_ma: You can combine smaller measures to make any common fraction.
missing.a_medical_thermometer_shows_tenths_of_: A medical thermometer shows tenths of a degree. 98.6°F = 98 + 6/10 = 98.6. A glass thermometer often shows half-degrees.
missing.each_minor_tick_is_usually_1_10_of_a_d: Each minor tick is usually 1/10 of a degree.
missing.most_fuel_gauges_show_divisions_at_e_e: Most fuel gauges show divisions at E (empty), 1/4, 1/2, 3/4, F (full). Modern digital gauges may show 1/8 increments or exact percentages.
missing.a_standard_large_pizza_is_sliced_into_: A standard large pizza is sliced into 8 (sometimes 6 or 10). Each slice is 1/8 (or 1/6, 1/10) of the whole.
missing.a_clock_is_a_12_hour_cycle_each_hour_i: A clock is a 12-hour cycle. Each hour is 1/12 of the cycle. 30 minutes = 1/2 hour. 15 minutes = 1/4 hour.
missing.there_are_60_minutes_in_an_hour_so_the: There are 60 minutes in an hour, so the denominator is 60.
missing.a_baseball_batting_average_like_333_me: A baseball batting average like .333 means the batter gets a hit 1/3 of the time. Free-throw percentage of .800 means 4 out of every 5 attempts go in.
missing.hits_over_at_bats_then_write_as_a_3_de: Hits over at-bats, then write as a 3-decimal-place average.
missing.where_you_actually_encounter_fractions: Where you actually encounter fractions in daily life. Browse tools, read them, and practice with realistic prompts.
missing.generate_curriculum_based_measurement_: Generate curriculum-based measurement probes for tracking student progress over time. Print, time, score, and graph the results to monitor RTI Tier 2 or Tier 3 students.
missing.earn_xp_from_correct_answers_and_badge: Earn XP from correct answers and badges to level up. Each level unlocks a new title.
missing.find_the_lcm_of_the_denominators: Find the LCM of the denominators.
missing.add_the_numerators_keep_the_denominato: Add the numerators. Keep the denominator.
missing.simplify_by_dividing_both_by_gcd: Simplify by dividing both by GCD.
missing.a_3_4_grid_with_2_rows_shaded_and_3_co: A 3×4 grid with 2 rows shaded and 3 columns shaded = 6 cells out of 12 = 1/2.
missing.dividing_a_fraction_by_a_fraction: Dividing a fraction by a fraction
missing.how_many_1_2_fit_in_3_4_3_4_is_one_and: "How many 1/2 fit in 3/4?" 3/4 is one-and-a-half halves. ✓
missing.find_the_gcd_of_the_numerator_and_deno: Find the GCD of the numerator and denominator.
missing.factors_of_18_1_2_3_6_9_18_factors_of_: Factors of 18: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18. Factors of 24: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 24. Common factors: 1, 2, 3, 6. GCD = 6.
missing.divide_top_and_bottom_by_the_gcd_2: Divide top and bottom by the GCD.
missing.multiply_numerator_by_the_same_number: Multiply numerator by the same number.
missing.5_8_is_10_8_yes_so_5_8_1_2_both_are_bi: 5/8: is 10 > 8? Yes. So 5/8 > 1/2. Both are bigger than 1/2 — need another check.
missing.each_friend_gets_5_18_of_the_original_: Each friend gets 5/18 of the original pizza.
missing.walk_through_each_procedure_one_step_a: Walk through each procedure one step at a time. Click through to see the reasoning behind each move. Research (Sweller) shows worked examples are more effective than practice problems alone for novice learners.
missing.common_questions_about_fractions_answe: Common questions about fractions, answered. Use this to settle confusion or as a starting point for student discussions.
missing.same_numerator_more_pieces_smaller_pie: Same numerator? More pieces = smaller pieces.
missing.there_is_more_than_one_way_to_compare_: There is more than one way to compare fractions. Knowing all five lets you pick the fastest method for each problem.
missing.quick_reference_card_for_fraction_oper: Quick-reference card for fraction operations and procedures. Save or print this for students.
missing.add_subtract_the_numerators_keep_the_d: Add/subtract the numerators. Keep the denominator.
missing.1_find_lcm_2_convert_both_fractions_3_: 1) Find LCM. 2) Convert both fractions. 3) Add/subtract. 4) Simplify.
missing.you_can_always_use_the_product_of_deno: You can always use the product of denominators as a common denominator (not always smallest, but it works).
missing.multiplying_by_1_makes_things_smaller_: Multiplying by < 1 makes things smaller. Multiplying by > 1 makes things bigger.
missing.keep_first_fraction_change_to_flip_sec: Keep first fraction. Change ÷ to ×. Flip second fraction (reciprocal).
missing.how_many_of_these_fit_in_that_4_1_2_ho: "How many of these fit in that?" 4 ÷ 1/2 = "how many halves in 4?" = 8.
missing.multiply_or_divide_top_and_bottom_by_t: Multiply (or divide) top AND bottom by the same nonzero number. Value stays the same.
missing.divide_top_and_bottom_by_their_gcd_gre: Divide top and bottom by their GCD (greatest common factor).
missing.cross_multiply_a_b_c_d_iff_a_d_b_c: Cross-multiply. a/b = c/d iff a×d = b×c.
missing.multiply_by_100_or_convert_to_decimal_: Multiply by 100 (or convert to decimal first).
missing.a_b_vs_c_d_a_d_vs_b_c: a/b vs c/d → a×d vs b×c.
missing.just_like_whole_number_fact_families_3: Just like whole-number fact families (3 + 4 = 7, 7 - 3 = 4, 7 - 4 = 3), fractions have fact families too.
missing.a_single_triple_generates_four_related: A single triple generates four related equations.
missing.quickly_estimate_which_benchmark_a_fra: Quickly estimate which benchmark a fraction is closest to. This is the fastest way to develop fraction number sense.
missing.probability_is_favorable_outcomes_tota: Probability is favorable outcomes / total outcomes. Move the sliders to see how probability and fractions are the same thing.
missing.for_english_language_learners_see_frac: For English Language Learners: see fraction vocabulary in your home language alongside English.
missing.bilingual_instruction_speeds_math_acqu: Bilingual instruction speeds math acquisition because the mathematical concept is universal — only the words change.
missing.a_hand_curated_set_of_practice_items_o: A hand-curated set of practice items organized by skill. Browse by category, or use them as a quick warmup before challenges.
missing.enter_two_fractions_and_an_operation_t: Enter two fractions and an operation. This calculator shows every step, not just the answer.
missing.fractions_make_patterns_build_a_color_: Fractions make patterns. Build a color wheel, a fraction grid, or a kaleidoscope.
missing.between_any_two_fractions_there_is_alw: Between any two fractions, there is always another fraction. Then another. And another, forever. This is called "density." Pick two fractions and find ones between them.
missing.add_the_fractions_and_divide_by_2: Add the fractions and divide by 2.
missing.add_the_tops_add_the_bottoms_this_is_n: Add the tops, add the bottoms. (This is NOT addition — it's the mediant.) Always between the two fractions.
missing.you_could_repeat_this_process_forever_: You could repeat this process forever, finding new fractions between every pair. There are infinitely many fractions between any two fractions.
missing.pick_a_recipe_and_scale_it_up_or_down_: Pick a recipe and scale it up or down using fraction multiplication. Every cooking decision becomes a fraction multiplication problem.
missing.real_problems_usually_take_multiple_st: Real problems usually take multiple steps. These chains walk you through 2-4 sub-questions building toward a final answer.
missing.where_fractions_come_from_surprising_c: Where fractions come from, surprising connections, and why mathematics is full of stories. Click any title to expand.
missing.look_up_materials_definitions_and_refe: Look-up materials, definitions, and reference guides.
missing.fun_extras_trivia_history_and_surprisi: Fun extras: trivia, history, and surprising math.
missing.play_with_the_math_without_a_quiz_form: Play with the math without a quiz format.
missing.timed_and_scored_practice_for_fluency: Timed and scored practice for fluency.
missing.curriculum_planning_standards_alignmen: Curriculum planning, standards alignment, and student goal-setting.
missing.pedagogical_resources_for_teaching_fra: Pedagogical resources for teaching fractions effectively.
missing.your_settings_progress_and_saved_work: Your settings, progress, and saved work.
missing.a_fraction_is_a_number_with_a_size_eac: A fraction is a number with a size. Each strip is one whole wide, so how far the color reaches is the value. Turn on the common-denominator grid to see equivalent fractions line up on the same line.
missing.no_strips_yet_add_one_above_or_tap_exa: No strips yet. Add one above, or tap Example to watch equivalent fractions line up.
missing.some_strips_show_a_different_number_of: ⚠ Some strips show a different number of parts but the SAME size. Equivalence means equal magnitude, not an equal count of pieces.
missing.plot_these_on_a_number_line: Plot these on a number line →
missing.common_core_fluency_automatic_recall_o: Common Core fluency = automatic recall of basic facts in under 3 seconds. Pizza model for what-out-of-the-whole; bar model for comparison and operation.
missing.three_tools_same_denom_just_compare_to: Three tools: same denom (just compare tops), cross-multiplication (a/b vs c/d → ad vs bc), or benchmark to ½ / ¾. Last one is fastest in your head.
missing.add_sub_common_denominator_first_multi: Add/sub: common denominator first. Multiply: tops×tops, bottoms×bottoms. Divide: keep-change-flip (multiply by reciprocal). Always simplify to lowest terms.
missing.multiply_top_and_bottom_by_the_same_nu: Multiply top AND bottom by the same number; value stays put. ½ = 2/4 = 50/100 = 0.5 = 50%. Equivalent fractions are the bridge between fractions, decimals, and percents.
missing.2_11_4_long_division_gives_a_terminati: 2¾ = 11/4. Long division gives a terminating decimal (denom = 2¹ × 5ⁿ) or a repeating one (any other prime in the denom). 1/3 = 0.333... forever.
missing.stack_the_fraction_wall_1_whole_2_halv: Stack the fraction wall: 1 whole = 2 halves = 4 quarters = 8 eighths. Same height = same value. Cuisenaire rods (1952) made this concrete; the wall is the digital descendant.
missing.mix_n_copies_of_a_fraction_discover_wh: Mix N copies of a fraction. Discover when they sum to a whole. No score, no reveal.
missing.hypothesis_when_do_n_copies_of_a_fract: Hypothesis: When do N copies of a fraction equal exactly 1?
missing.what_relationship_between_count_and_de: What relationship between count and denominator gives exactly 1?
missing.find_two_combinations_that_produce_und: Find two combinations that produce "under". What do they share?
missing.explain_how_count_num_den_determines_w: Explain how count × (num/den) determines whether you reach 1.
missing.design_note_discrete_3_state_outcome_n: Design note: discrete 3-state outcome; no exact-match score; no reveal — by design.
missing.1_6_tabs_n_new_challenge_b_benchmarks_: ⌨️ 1-6: tabs | N: new challenge | B: benchmarks | P: pie/bar | ?: AI tutor
missing.equivalent_fractions_same_value_differ: 🍰 Equivalent Fractions — Same value, different forms
